questions.q: A beam of light consisting of two wavelengths 6000Å and 8000Å is used to obtain interference fringes in a young's double slit experiment. The distance between the slits is 0.15mm. Find the distance of the 15th bright fringe from the central maximum.
questions.answer: The distance of the 15th bright fringe from the central maximum is 15λD/d = 15 * 6000 * 10^(-10) * 0.5m / 0.15 * 10^(-3) = 2m.

questions.q: In a lens, the focal length of the combination of two lenses separated by a distance of 100cm is 100cm. The focal length of one lens is 50 cm. What is the focal length of the other lens?
questions.answer: The focal length of the other lens is -200cm.

questions.q: A compound microscope has an objective of focal length 2mm and an eyepiece of focal length 5cm. The distance between the objective and eyepiece is 15cm. Calculate the magnifying power for normal adjustment.
questions.answer: The magnifying power for normal adjustment is (25 * 25 * 4) = 2500.

questions.q: True or False: In a compound microscope, the eyepiece is placed at the least distance of distinct vision.

In a Young's double slit experiment, if the fringe width is 1mm, find the distance of the 10th dark fringe from the central maximum when the screen is 50cm away. Take wavelength of the light to be 5000Å.
questions.answer: The distance of the 10th dark fringe from the central maximum is (10 - 0.5) * 1 * 10^(-3) * 50 = 0.475m.

questions.q: Fill in the blanks: In a telescope, the objective lens is always placed at the focus of the eyepiece lens.
A convex lens of focal length 20cm forms a real and inverted image of a candle flame on a screen placed at a distance of 60cm from the lens. Find the distance of the candle flame from the lens.
questions.answer: The distance of the candle flame from the lens is 40cm.

questions.q: In a compound microscope, if the tube length is 16cm, the focal length of the objective is 2mm, and the focal length of the eyepiece is 5cm, find the magnifying power for normal adjustment.
questions.answer: The magnifying power for normal adjustment is (16 * 25 * 4) = 1600.

questions.q: A plane mirror produces a virtual, erect and laterally inverted image of an object. State the nature of the image formed by a convex lens.
questions.answer: The image formed by a convex lens is real and inverted.

questions.q: A telescope has an objective lens of focal length 100cm and an eyepiece of focal length 5cm. Find the angular magnification of the telescope.
questions.answer: The angular magnification of the telescope is 20.
